Root cause of manifestLoadError: HLS.js fetched /api/tv/stream without the

- Frontend reads stream progressively: spinner hides on first source,
  more sources appear in the source bar as they arrive
- Hard cap 15s (was 10s), per-site timeout stays 5s
- Deduplication by URL on the server side

fix(nu): gracefully skip init when oh-my-posh is not installed (#7442)

* fix(nu): add early return guard when oh-my-posh is not available

If a user uninstalls oh-my-posh without removing the autoload script,
the script would still run on every Nu startup and produce errors.

Add an early return guard at the top of omp.nu that exits the script
immediately if the executable is no longer available. Handles both
install modes:
- Absolute path (standard install): \path exists\
- Base name only (MSIX / --strict mode): \which\

Fixes #7245

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(msi): accept WiX v7 EULA in build script

WiX v7 now requires explicit EULA acceptance under the Open Source
Maintenance Fee policy. Add -acceptEula wix7 to the wix build command
so CI releases don't fail.

See: https://docs.firegiant.com/wix/osmf/#direct-acceptance

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(nu): guard path exists check to absolute/relative paths only

When the executable is stored as a bare name (e.g. MSIX / --strict mode),
\path exists\ resolves against the current working directory and may
incorrectly return true if an unrelated file with the same name exists
there. Only apply the \path exists\ check when the executable string
looks like a path (contains a separator or starts with . / ~); otherwise
rely solely on \which\ for the PATH-based lookup.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix dynamic Function new.target leak and generator callable dispatch

- Add push_dynamic_function_call_new_target() helper to isolate
  new.target for BUILTIN_CTOR_FUNCTION/BUILTIN_NEW_FUNCTION calls,
  preventing outer constructor new.target from leaking into
  CreateDynamicFunction and corrupting the prototype chain.

- Fix __fn_body__ callable_expr to use function*/async function/
  async function* for generator/async dynamic functions.

- Split eval+call for generators: eval the function expression first,
  then call it separately so the generator's bytecode outlives the
  eval scope (fixes generators returning {done:true} immediately).

- Route all dynamic generator calls in runner.rs through
  vm_call_function_value instead of manual yield parsing, fixing
  return value handling in dynamic generators.

Cluster 32: 581→595 pass (+14), Cluster 29: 701→704 pass (+3)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(hostexec): resolve relative argv0 against cwd for rule matching

When an agent runs `cd backend && ./gradlew spotlessApply`, the Python
wrapper sends argv0="./gradlew" with cwd="/workspace/backend". The rule
defines argv0="./backend/gradlew" (workspace-root-relative). matchRule
compared these directly and failed to match, causing a fallback error:
"relative argv0 fallback is not supported".

Fix matchRule to accept an optional MatchContext (cwd + workspaceRoot).
When direct relative-path comparison fails, resolve the actual argv0
against cwd to an absolute path, then re-derive the workspace-relative
path for comparison. This is done in the broker (TS) where both cwd and
workspaceRoot are already available, avoiding changes to the Python
wrapper or new environment variables.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Skip secondary vtable emission on COFF path to fix LNK2005 duplicate RTTI symbols

Secondary vtables (with non-zero offset_to_top) are an Itanium/ELF concept.
On the COFF/MSVC path, emitting them causes duplicate RTTI symbols like
??_R1.?AVDerived@@8 because ObjFileWriter::add_vtable unconditionally emits
RTTI structures keyed by class_name without deduplication.

* Fix use-after-move: defer vtable_info move until after secondary vtable registration

* Guard secondary vptr stores with Itanium ABI check to fix LNK2019 on COFF

The emitVptrStores lambda was emitting MemberStore ops referencing
$flash$vtable$ secondary vtable symbols on both ELF and COFF paths.
These symbols are only generated by the ELF vtable emission code,
causing LNK2019 unresolved externals on Windows/COFF.

* Fix COFF virtual base construction

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Simplify collectReachableVBases: avoid redundant traversal of virtual_bases and base_classes

Since StructTypeInfo::virtual_bases already contains all transitively
reachable virtual bases, we only need to iterate virtual_bases for
collection and recurse through non-virtual bases in base_classes.
Remove the redundant virtual-base collection from base_classes and
the unnecessary recursion into virtual bases (which are shared and
already fully captured by the virtual_bases list).

* Fix use-after-move: save ctor_decl_op.mangled_name before std::move

* Add TODO comment documenting missing destructor thunks in secondary vtables

* Add known issue: remove default ConstructorVariant parameters in NameMangling.h

commit.message:
feat: add eslint-plugin-react-you-might-not-need-an-effect (#988)

Adds
[`eslint-plugin-react-you-might-not-need-an-effect`](https://github.com/nickjvandyke/eslint-plugin-react-you-might-not-need-an-effect)
to catch unnecessary `useEffect` usage — derived state, chained state
updates, event handlers in effects, and similar anti-patterns.

## Changes

- **`package.json`**: adds
`eslint-plugin-react-you-might-not-need-an-effect` as a dev dependency
- **`eslint.config.js`**: imports and spreads the plugin's `recommended`
flat config

```js
import reactYouMightNotNeedAnEffect from "eslint-plugin-react-you-might-not-need-an-effect"

export default [
  // ...
  reactYouMightNotNeedAnEffect.configs.recommended,
  // ...
]
```

Enables 9 rules as warnings covering: `no-derived-state`,
`no-chain-state-updates`, `no-event-handler`, `no-initialize-state`,
`no-pass-data-to-parent`, `no-pass-live-state-to-parent`,
`no-adjust-state-on-prop-change`, `no-reset-all-state-on-prop-change`,
`no-empty-effect`.

* Default Windows PTY mode to RequireProxy

The in-process ConPTY path (Direct) starts processes correctly but
does not relay output to the terminal buffer, causing 6 test failures
in CI. Default to RequireProxy so the out-of-process hex1bpty.exe

reliably. Direct remains available as an explicit opt-in.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Isolate PTY shim files into runtimes subdirectory

The CopyWindowsPtyShim target was copying all files from the PtyHost
publish output (including Microsoft.Extensions.*.dll for net8.0) into
the test output root. This overwrote the net10.0 versions needed by
McpServer tests, causing assembly load failures.

Fix: copy shim output to runtimes/<rid>/native/ subdirectory, which
the WindowsPtyShimLocator already probes. This isolates the PtyHost's
dependencies from the test runner's assembly resolution.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix concurrent modification in TreeNode measure/render

TreeNode.MeasureCore and Render iterated FlattenedItems directly,
which could be modified by async expansion callbacks running on
the reconciliation path. Snapshot the list before iterating.

commit.message:
fix: emit FTCS_COMMAND_EXECUTED (OSC 133;C) in PowerShell with shell integration (#7441)

* fix: emit FTCS_COMMAND_EXECUTED (OSC 133;C) in PowerShell when shell integration is enabled

When shell_integration is enabled without transient/streaming, PowerShell
never called Enable-KeyHandlers, so the Enter key handler that emits
\e]133;C\a (FTCS_COMMAND_EXECUTED) was never registered.

Enable KeyHandlers for PowerShell whenever FTCSMarks (shell integration)
is active so terminals like Ghostty can measure command execution time.

Bash, Zsh, and Fish are unaffected  they emit FTCS_C via preexec/PS0
hooks independently of the key handler feature.

Fixes #7377

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Replace real GitHub API calls with a local httptest.Server and make
http.IsConnected a mockable function variable so TestCanUpgrade is

connectivity issues.

commit.message:
fix: Enable testTagsAsResourceId for Maestro id: selectors (#308)

Adds testTagsAsResourceId = true to Scaffold's semantics modifier
to expose Compose testTags as resource IDs in the accessibility tree.
This allows Maestro flows to find elements using id: selectors
(e.g., id: 'nav_history', id: 'search_bar').

Without this, Maestro's id: selector cannot find testTag elements
because Compose does not expose testTags as resource-id by default.

Fixes #307

Co-authored-by: Copilot <copilot@github.com>
